blocks.body: Third Conditional helps you talk about an unreal past with if + past perfect, would have + V3.

{"level":"B2","intro":"The Third Conditional describes an unreal past situation: we imagine that events happened differently and talk about a different past result.","rules":[{"label":"PAST CONDITION","color":"emerald","text":"The if-clause usually uses the Past Perfect.","example":"If we had left earlier, we would have caught the train."},{"label":"PAST RESULT","color":"blue","text":"The main clause most often uses would have + V3.","example":"If she had studied more, she would have passed."},{"label":"REGRET AND ANALYSIS","color":"amber","text":"This form often expresses regret, criticism, or analysis after the event.","example":"If I had listened, I wouldn't have made that mistake."}]}

{"title":"1. Form","intro":"The core pattern is if + past perfect, would have + V3.","items":[{"label":"Main pattern","title":"if + had + V3, would have + V3","subtitle":"If he had called, I would have answered."},{"label":"Result first","title":"would have + V3 + if + clause","subtitle":"I would have helped if I had known."},{"label":"Negative form","title":"if hadn't + V3","subtitle":"If they hadn't missed the bus, they would have been on time."},{"label":"Other modals","title":"could have / might have","subtitle":"If you had asked, I might have joined."}]}

{"title":"2. Main uses","sections":[{"label":"Past regret","color":"emerald","description":"We regret that something did not happen differently in the past.","examples":[{"en":"If I had left home earlier, I would have caught the plane.","ua":"If I had left home earlier, I would have caught the plane."},{"en":"If she had taken the map, she wouldn't have got lost.","ua":"If she had taken the map, she wouldn't have got lost."}]},{"label":"Imagined alternative result","color":"blue","description":"We compare the real chain of events with a different imagined past result.","examples":[{"en":"If we had booked sooner, we would have paid less.","ua":"If we had booked sooner, we would have paid less."},{"en":"If they had listened to the warning, they would have avoided the problem.","ua":"If they had listened to the warning, they would have avoided the problem."}]},{"label":"Criticism or after-event analysis","color":"amber","description":"The pattern often appears when we analyse a mistake or consequence after the fact.","examples":[{"en":"If you had checked the file, you would have seen the typo.","ua":"If you had checked the file, you would have seen the typo."},{"en":"If I had known about the delay, I would have stayed at home longer.","ua":"If I had known about the delay, I would have stayed at home longer."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Putting would have in the if-clause.","wrong":"If I would have known, I would have called.","right":"If I had known, I would have called.","hint":"In the Third Conditional, the if-clause normally uses the past perfect."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using past simple instead of past perfect.","wrong":"If she studied more, she would have passed.","right":"If she had studied more, she would have passed.","hint":"For an unreal past condition, use had + V3."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing Third Conditional with present regret.","wrong":"If I had more money yesterday, I would be happier now.","right":"If I had had more money yesterday, I would have been happier then.","hint":"The Third Conditional focuses on a past cause and a past result. If the result is present, you probably need a mixed conditional."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick rule","items":["Use if + past perfect, would have + V3 for an unreal past situation.","This form often expresses regret, afterthought, or an alternative result.","Do not normally put would have in the if-clause.","If a past cause leads to a present result, you probably need a mixed conditional."]}
